action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 18 achieved 81.89% average accuracy with LBFGS, still below the best of 83.11% obtained in iteration 10 using AdamW + cosine head + full feature replay + per-task head reinit + TTA. Analysis: The LBFGS optimizer converges to near-zero training loss but generalizes worse than the properly-regularized AdamW recipe (label smoothing 0.1, feature noise 0.01, cosine annealing stepped per epoch). The current train.py is the LBFGS version. I need to revert to the proven AdamW recipe, increasing epochs from 20 to 30 to squeeze extra accuracy, while keeping the same IN21k ViT backbone and TTA predict.py. Decision: Rewrite train.py with clean AdamW loop, per-epoch cosine annealing, label smoothing, feature noise, and best-head selection per task. Run training + verify predict.py, then immediately submit eval.
